name: Psyllium

book_claims.claim: Part of cholesterol-lowering food portfolio reducing LDL-cholesterol by 13%
book_claims.mechanism: Water-soluble fibre blocks reabsorption of cholesterol and bile acids in small intestine
Soluble fibre feeds beneficial gut bacteria, produces anti-inflammatory short-chain fatty acids; directly modulates intestinal immune response

book_claims.claim: Supplementation causes substantial improvement in gastrointestinal symptoms in ulcerative colitis patients in remission
book_claims.mechanism: Prebiotic fibre supports beneficial bacterial populations and SCFA production; reduces intestinal inflammation

supplementary_research.finding: Meta-analysis of 21 RCTs found psyllium supplementation reduced LDL cholesterol by 7% and total cholesterol by 5%. Effects were dose-dependent and consistent across studies, supporting the book's cholesterol-lowering claims.

Psyllium's gel-forming soluble fibre is the only fibre supplement with sufficient evidence to earn FDA-approved health claims for both cholesterol lowering and blood glucose reduction. Forms a viscous gel that slows digestion and traps bile acids.

Psyllium supplementation significantly increased Faecalibacterium (butyrate-producing bacteria) abundance in healthy adults, supporting its prebiotic role in maintaining gut barrier integrity and reducing intestinal inflammation.
Strong evidence for cholesterol reduction (LDL -5-10%), blood glucose management, and improved bowel regularity. FDA-approved health claim for coronary heart disease risk reduction.

culinary_pairings.source: Both soluble fibre sources; combined in breakfast porridge for cholesterol lowering
Blends seamlessly into fruit and vegetable smoothies
Mixed into yogurt for added fibre without flavour change
Different fibre types feed complementary gut bacteria populations
Cinnamon masks psyllium's mild earthiness in drinks and porridge
Combined soluble fibre sources for comprehensive gut and cholesterol support

seasonality: Available year-round as dried husk supplement. Plantago ovata grown primarily in India. No seasonal variation in supplemental form.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Forms viscous gel in gut that traps bile acids, slows glucose absorption, and feeds beneficial bacteria. Must be taken with adequate water (250ml+ per 5g dose).

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: N/A (produced by gut bacteria from psyllium fermentation)
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Gut bacteria ferment psyllium to produce butyrate, propionate, and acetate. Butyrate is the primary energy source for colonocytes and has anti-inflammatory properties.

synergies.description: Both are soluble fibre sources in the cholesterol-lowering food portfolio
Different fibre types (soluble psyllium vs mixed legume fibre) feed complementary gut bacteria populations
